rag_rules: Fix unresolved references by using only visible identifiers from the current component scope; do not introduce new architecture.
Ensure type consistency in AGREE expressions: numeric variables must have numeric expressions, Boolean variables must have Boolean expressions.
Move implementation-level bindings from component type annexes to implementation annexes when necessary.
Delete invalid AGREE clauses that reference unresolved identifiers instead of adding missing architecture.
generic_rules: Validator diagnostics pointing to unresolved identifiers should be resolved by referencing only existing visible symbols.
AGREE expressions must maintain type consistency; avoid mixing incompatible types like int and <error>.
Component type contracts should only reference features available at that scope; implementation-level details go in implementation annexes.
When AGREE reports type mismatches, align helper declarations with the actual expression types (int, bool, real).
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Use only declared constants, equations, or visible ports in AGREE expressions.
Helper functions and variables must be defined within the same annex or imported.
Implementation-level assignments should be placed in implementation scopes, not component type scopes.
anti_patterns: Do not attempt to resolve missing identifiers by inventing new declarations or architecture elements.
Avoid mixing incompatible types in AGREE expressions, such as assigning an int to a <error> type.
Do not place implementation-level references in component type annexes unless they are valid variables or outputs.
